outcomes: Understand nature of matter, properties, measurement, uncertainty, laws of combination, atomic theory
Calculate mole concept, molar masses, percentage composition, stoichiometry
Represent chemical phenomena at macroscopic, molecular, and symbolic levels
Trace discoveries of sub-atomic particles and atomic models
Explain developments leading to Bohr's Model for Hydrogen Atom
Describe the Quantum Mechanical Model of the Atom

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
outcomes: Explain the need for classification and genesis of periodic classification
Apply Modern Periodic Law and nomenclature rules (Z > 100)
Correlate electronic configuration with position in table and element type (s, p, d, f blocks)
Analyze periodic trends in properties
Apply Kossel-Lewis approach for Ionic/Electrovalent bonds
Analyze bond parameters and hybridization using Valence Shell Electron Pair Repulsion (VSEPR)
Explain bonding via Valence Bond Theory and Molecular Orbital Theory
Describe Hydrogen bonding in homonuclear diatomic molecules
Measure ∆U and ∆H via calorimetry
Calculate Enthalpy Change for different reaction types
Determine spontaneity of reactions using Gibbs Energy change
Define Dynamic Equilibrium and Law of Chemical Equilibrium
Distinguish between Homogeneous and Heterogeneous equilibria
Relate K, Q, and Gibbs Energy (G)
Analyze factors affecting equilibrium in ionic solutions, acids/bases, buffers, and solubility
Classify Redox Reactions based on Oxidation/Reduction concepts
Analyze redox processes in terms of electron transfer
Assign and calculate Oxidation Numbers

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques
learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques
outcomes: Represent organic compounds structurally based on shapes of bonds
Apply IUPAC nomenclature rules for classification
Identify types of Isomerism
Analyze fundamental concepts in Organic Reaction Mechanisms
Classify Alkanes, Alkenes, Alkynes, and Aromatic Hydrocarbons
Analyze carcinogenic nature and toxicity of specific compounds
